seoTitle: How to Run a Script in Airtable, Create Automation
metaDescription: Write and deploy custom JavaScript in Airtable Automations using the Run a Script action, with input variables and the built-in test tool.
intro: For workflows that go beyond Airtable's standard automation actions, the Run a Script step lets you write custom JavaScript that operates directly on your record data. This guide covers setting up the trigger, passing input variables into your code, and using the live test feature to debug before going live.

steps.heading: Open the Automations tab
steps.body: Navigate to the Automations directory within your Airtable base.

steps.heading: Create a new automation and trigger
steps.body: Start a new automation and define your initial trigger event, such as a record being created or updated.

steps.heading: Add the Run a Script action
steps.body: Add a new action component and select the 'Run a script' developer tool from the list of available actions.

steps.heading: Configure input variables
steps.body: Define input variables to pass dynamic data, like active record IDs, directly into your scripting environment.

steps.heading: Write your JavaScript logic
steps.body: Type your custom JavaScript code into the integrated code editor, using the input variables you configured.

steps.heading: Test and activate
steps.body: Click the Test button to debug errors and confirm output success, then toggle the automation on to run it live.

faq.question: Do I need coding experience to use the Run a Script action?
faq.answer: Yes, this action requires writing JavaScript, so some coding familiarity is needed compared to Airtable's no-code automation steps.

faq.question: Can scripts read and write data to my Airtable base?
faq.answer: Yes, the scripting environment provides access to your base's tables and records so scripts can read, create, or update data.

faq.question: How do I pass data into my script?
faq.answer: You define input variables in the action's configuration, which map dynamic values like record IDs into your script's runtime.

faq.question: What happens if my script has an error?
faq.answer: The Test feature will surface errors so you can debug your code before turning the automation on for live use.
